steps.title: Check if the equations are scalar multiples of each other
steps.content:
Divide the second equation's coefficients and constant by those from the first equation:

$$\frac{17.5}{3.5} = 5$$

$$\frac{6}{1.2} = 5$$

$$\frac{40}{8} = 5$$

steps.title: Identify the solution type
steps.content: Since all three ratios equal 5, the second equation is exactly 5 times the first equation. This means both equations represent the same line. Any point on this line is a solution to both equations, so there are infinitely many solutions. That matches choice C — C is correct.
